analysis.note: Adjectival compound agreeing with vijñānam; translated “all-seeded.”

verification.detail: compound stem sarvabīja as Napumsaka/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation bahuvrihi: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Nominative subject of the verbless first clause, with sarvabījam as predicate/adjectival complement.
Nominative subject of yāti; “its” in the translation is supplied from the preceding vijñāna, not from an expressed genitive.
First member of the repeated adverbial expression tathā tathā, “in such-and-such ways.”
Second member of the repeated adverbial expression tathā tathā.
Finite verb: “goes/proceeds.” The supplied dictionary excerpt lists yā as a noun, but the form yāti is the standard present of √yā, as also identified by the analyzer.
Ablative of cause/source: “from, because of, under” mutual control or dependence.

verification.detail: compound stem anyonyavaśa as Pum/Panchami/Eka: pass; relation tatpurusa: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Instrumental relative pronoun, construed with pariṇāmaḥ/process: “by which/whereby.” The form could also be neuter; masculine is chosen by reference to pariṇāmaḥ.
Nominative subject of jāyate; translated contextually as “conceptual construction.”
First member of the repeated demonstrative sa sa, giving a distributive sense, “this or that / each corresponding.”
Second member of the repeated demonstrative sa sa.
Ātmanepada form in intransitive kartari sense, “arises/is born”; not treated here as a Sanskrit passive.

justifications.decision: The neuter nominative singular sarvabījam agrees with neuter nominative singular vijñānam. With MW s.v. sarva, “all, every,” and MW s.v. bīja, “seed” and “germ, primary cause or principle,” the compound is best taken as possessive/adjectival: consciousness is furnished with all seeds, not as an independent object “all seeds.”
justifications.chosen: sarvabījam = “all-seeded,” a bahuvrīhi adjective of vijñānam.

justifications.decision: The form pariṇāmaḥ is masculine nominative singular, and yāti is intransitive. This favors taking pariṇāmaḥ as the subject of the finite verb rather than as an accusative goal/object. The connection with vijñānam is contextual: the transformation is the transformation of the previously named consciousness.
options_considered: pariṇāmaḥ as predicate noun in apposition to vijñānam
justifications.chosen: pariṇāmaḥ is the nominative subject of yāti: “transformation proceeds.”

justifications.decision: MW s.v. tathā gives “in that manner, so, thus”; repetition naturally yields a distributive/variegated sense, coordinated with the later repeated demonstrative sa sa.
justifications.chosen: tathā tathā is a repeated adverbial: “in such-and-such ways.”

justifications.decision: MW s.v. anyonya gives “one another, mutual,” and MW s.v. vaśa gives “authority, power, control.” The ablative singular -āt is therefore causal/source-like: the proceeding occurs because of, or under, mutual dependence/control.
options_considered: literal possessive: “from the will of one another”
justifications.chosen: anyonyavaśāt = “from mutual influence/dependence,” an ablative tatpuruṣa.

justifications.decision: The instrumental relative pronoun links the preceding process of transformation with the arising of vikalpa. Since pariṇāmaḥ is masculine singular, masculine instrumental yena is a plausible agreement target, though the form is also neuter in isolation.
options_considered: neuter adverbial: “whereby” referring to the whole preceding process
relative introducing a separate unspecified antecedent

evidence.translation: whereby this or that conceptual construction arises

justifications.decision: The repeated masculine nominative singular demonstrative agrees with vikalpaḥ and gives a distributive series of particular constructions rather than one single repeated item.
options_considered: distributive demonstrative: “each such / this or that vikalpa”
emphatic demonstrative with no distributive force
justifications.chosen: sa sa is distributive: “this or that / each corresponding.”

evidence.translation: this or that conceptual construction arises

justifications.decision: Although jāyate is middle in form and can be rendered idiomatically “is born,” it functions here as an intransitive present of √jan with vikalpaḥ as nominative subject. The instrumental yena supplies cause/means, not a grammatical passive agent.
justifications.chosen: jāyate is kartari/intransitive: “arises,” not a passive “is produced” with an expressed agent.

evidence.translation: this or that conceptual construction arises

one_shot_delta: Without commentary, sarvabījam could easily be flattened into the noun phrase “all seeds”; the grammar here supports taking it as an adjective of vijñānam, “all-seeded.”
A commentary-blind reading may make vijñānam the subject of yāti; the nominative pariṇāmaḥ plus intransitive yāti more straightforwardly yields “transformation proceeds.”

The technical force of vikalpa is vulnerable: a dictionary-only rendering might say “option/alternative,” while the Yogācāra context favors “conceptual construction,” though this ablation run cannot confirm that from bhāṣya evidence.
analyzer_disagreements: The analyzer split sarva_bījam as sarva + bījam; I treat sarvabījam as one inflected compound stem, as required for compound analysis and supported by its agreement with vijñānam.
The analyzer split anyonya_vaśāt as anyonya + vaśāt; I treat anyonyavaśāt as one inflected compound stem, since it functions as a single ablative causal expression.
open_questions: No commentary span was supplied in this ablation run, so the exact bhāṣya-supported force of sarvabīja, vikalpa, and the antecedent-scope of yena remains provisional.
